name: AI Browser
description: Hecos AI-controlled Playwright browser. Navigate, read DOM, click elements and fill forms without vision or coordinate guessing.
routing_instructions: BROWSER CONTROL DECISION TREE — Follow these steps in order: (1) ALWAYS start with BROWSER__list_tabs. (2) If it returns a list of tabs → you are in CDP mode. Use BROWSER__switch_tab(index) to go to the right tab, then use BROWSER page tools (get_page_text, click_element, type_in_field). (3) If list_tabs returns 'CDP connection is not active' → Chrome was NOT launched in AI-Ready mode. IMMEDIATELY fall back to: AUTOMATION__get_browser_tabs to find open tabs by title (this reads from Windows accessibility, real browser windows only), then AUTOMATION__focus_browser_tab with the matching title fragment. NEVER stop and ask the user — always attempt the fallback automatically.

commands.open_url: Navigate the browser to a URL.
commands.go_back: Navigate back to the previous page.
commands.get_page_text: Read all visible text from the current page.
commands.get_links: List all hyperlinks on the page.
commands.click_element: Click an element by visible text or aria-label.
commands.type_in_field: Fill a form field by label/placeholder and optionally press Enter.
commands.scroll: Scroll the page up or down.
commands.press_key: Press a keyboard key in the browser.
commands.run_js: Execute JavaScript code on the page.
commands.screenshot: Take a viewport screenshot for vision analysis.
commands.list_tabs: List all open tabs in the browser (useful for CDP mode).
commands.switch_tab: Switch focus to a specific tab by its ID.

function.name: BROWSER__open_url
function.description: Opens a URL in the Hecos AI browser. The browser window will appear automatically. Use this instead of the WEB plugin when you need to interact with a page.

function.name: BROWSER__get_current_url
function.description: Returns the URL currently loaded in the Hecos browser.

function.name: BROWSER__go_back
function.description: Navigate the browser back to the previous page.

function.name: BROWSER__get_page_text
function.description: Returns all visible text from the current browser page. Use after open_url to understand the page before interacting.

function.name: BROWSER__get_links
function.description: Returns all hyperlinks (label → URL) on the current page as a numbered list.

function.name: BROWSER__get_inputs
function.description: Lists all input fields, buttons and form elements on the current page. Use to identify fields before calling type_in_field.

function.name: BROWSER__get_title
function.description: Returns the title of the current browser page.

function.name: BROWSER__click_element
function.description: Clicks a page element by its visible text label or aria-label. Intelligent fallback to CSS selectors. Prefer this over coordinate-based clicking.

text_or_selector.description: Visible text, aria-label, or CSS selector of the element to click.

function.name: BROWSER__type_in_field
function.description: Finds an input field by its label/placeholder and types text into it. Optionally presses Enter to submit.

label_or_selector.description: Label, placeholder, aria-label or CSS selector of the input field.

press_enter.description: If true, presses Enter after typing. Useful for search boxes. Default: false.

function.name: BROWSER__scroll
function.description: Scrolls the browser page up or down.

function.name: BROWSER__press_key
function.description: Presses a keyboard key in the browser context (e.g. 'Enter', 'Escape', 'Tab', 'ArrowDown').

key.description: Key name following web standards, e.g. 'Enter', 'ArrowDown', 'Escape'.

function.name: BROWSER__run_js
function.description: Executes JavaScript in the browser and returns the result. Powerful for extracting data or triggering actions not accessible via DOM.

code.description: JavaScript expression or statement, e.g. 'document.title' or 'document.querySelector("h1").innerText'.

function.name: BROWSER__screenshot
function.description: Takes a screenshot of the current browser viewport. Returns the file path which the vision AI can analyze. Use only when text-based tools are insufficient.

function.name: BROWSER__list_tabs
function.description: Returns a list of all open tabs/pages in the current browser. Use in CDP mode to find existing sites like YouTube.

function.name: BROWSER__switch_tab
function.description: Switches the active browser focus to a specific tab by its ID.

index.description: Numeric ID of the tab to focus (from list_tabs).

function.name: BROWSER__close
function.description: Closes the Hecos AI browser window.
